title: Cross-region queue replay storm after consumer offset failover regression
summary: A regional failover event led to duplicated event processing, runaway backpressure, and delayed customer-visible workflows across analytics and notifications. The incident initially looked like a broker outage or a producer bug, but the RCA requires correlating failover ordering, consumer offset replay semantics, dedup cache saturation, and lag propagation across multiple downstream services.
operational_context: A traffic shift to the secondary region started at 06:11Z after network instability alerts in the primary region.
The ingestion consumer deployment at 05:58Z modified checkpoint persistence to speed failover recovery.
